_meta.ias_note: IAS officers belong to state cadres but are appointed by UPSC and routinely deputed to the Center (DoPT manages central deputation). The number below is the cadre's approved strength — at any time ~25–40% may be on Central deputation, leave, training, or vacant. AGMUT (Arunachal-Goa-Mizoram-Union Territories) and joint cadres are noted where applicable.
_meta.employees_note: State government direct employees in lakh — excludes Centrally-sponsored staff, PSU workers, and outsourced/contract workers (which can add 30–60% more depending on state).
_meta.corruption_source: CMS-India India Corruption Study 2019 — % of households reporting they paid a bribe to access a public service in the past 12 months. Some smaller states use regional averages where state-specific data is unavailable.
_meta.departments_note: Back-office = secretariat, administration, public works payroll-heavy units with low direct public output. Public-facing = where citizens actually interact with the state. Most state budgets show back-office salary bills crowding out public-facing capex.

dept_back.name: General Administration (Mantralaya)
dept_back.note: Apex secretariat — ~12,000 staff; bulk salary bill, slow file movement.

dept_back.name: Public Works
dept_back.note: Tender-heavy; recurring CAG audit findings on irregularities.

dept_back.name: Revenue & Forests
dept_back.note: Land records modernisation pending despite decades of effort.

dept_public.name: Public Health
dept_public.note: Runs ~1,800 PHCs and the BMC's flagship municipal hospital network.

dept_public.name: School Education
dept_public.note: ~1.1 lakh govt schools; outcomes lag Tamil Nadu and Kerala.

dept_public.name: Cooperation, Marketing & Textiles
dept_public.note: Sugar coops + handloom — political patronage channel as much as economic vehicle.

dept_back.note: Fort St. George; ~15,000 staff; oldest secretariat by continuous operation.

dept_back.note: Big capex but high cost-overrun rate flagged by CAG.

dept_back.name: Hindu Religious & Charitable Endowments
dept_back.note: Manages ~38,000 temples' wealth; recurring transparency disputes.
